docs.title: Courses
docs.text:
• Computation Structures

    6.004  →  6.1910 · In progress

    Digital circuits · ISA · single-cycle / pipelined RISC-V

    Branch · Bilibili video

  • Constructive Computer Architecture

    6.175  →  6.1920 · In progress

    Microarchitecture · pipelining · caches · multicore

    Branch · csg.csail.mit.edu/6.175

  • Computer System Architecture

    6.375  →  6.5900 · In progress

    Memory hierarchy · OoO execution · vector & GPU

    Branch · csg.csail.mit.edu/6.375

Recommended sequence

After learning BSV, the recommended sequence is: 6.175 Labs 0-4, 6.375 Labs 1-4, 6.175 Labs 5-8 and Proj 1-2, then the remaining 6.375 Lab 5.

If course as finished, the README entry is highlighted and the issue comment becomes a celebration block.

docs.text:
Computation Structures · 6.004  →  6.1910 · Resource · Branch

13 commit(s) on branch 6.1910. A commit titled completed: all done. marks the course as finished.

May 14, 2026 09:58 6.1910 21287fa learn: note that I have read the of...

plan: write and study rsnikhil/Bluespec_BSV_Tutorial(tutorial/) for hands-on practice at the next step.

Signed-off-by: Leo Cheng <chengkelfanke@gmail.com>

+0 −0 0A · 0M · 0D May 13, 2026 23:35 6.1910 941c212 baseline: v0.0.1 — env ready, learn...

Signed-off-by: heke1228 <chengkelfan@qq.com>

+0 −0 0A · 0M · 0D 20:43 6.1910 8b65388 rename: bsc submodule → specs

Move the B-Lang-org/bsc submodule from bsc/ to specs/ so the path reflects its actual role on this branch — a read-only stash of Bluespec specs and language reference for offline lookup, not a buildable bsc checkout per se.

The submodule URL and pinned commit (f013e614) are unchanged; only the working-tree path moves. Existing clones can run git submodule sync && git submodule update --init specs to migrate their local checkouts.

Signed-off-by: heke1228 <chengkelfan@qq.com>

+2 −2 0A · 2M · 0D 20:40 6.1910 f564caf add: bsc submodule for offline refe...

Pin B-Lang-org/bsc as a git submodule at bsc/ so the official Bluespec compiler source, language docs (bsc/doc/), and examples (bsc/examples/) are always reachable from this branch.

Clone-recursive on this branch will now bring down the compiler tree alongside the rsnikhil/Bluespec_BSV_Tutorial snapshot.

Signed-off-by: heke1228 <chengkelfan@qq.com>

+4 −0 2A · 0M · 0D 19:14 6.1910 0cec5f3 fixed: readme info. +1 −1 0A · 1M · 0D 18:46 6.1910 ea92bea fmt: readme. +0 −10 0A · 1M · 0D 18:42 6.1910 fd90640 fixed: readme tutorial. +1 −1 0A · 1M · 0D 16:51 6.1910 22a708d delete: example file +0 −34 0A · 0M · 1D May 12, 2026 12:28 6.1910 8df861a tests: lab01 scratch notes — adders...

First substantive entry for 6.1910. Drafted while reviewing the lab spec, partly to exercise the issue-comment / timeline / dashboard rendering with a real body + markdown formatting.

Highlights captured in the notes file:

  • Full adder gate-level breakdown (XOR/AND/OR composition).

  • Ripple-carry critical-path scaling — why O(N) delay falls apart past toy widths.

  • Carry-lookahead G / P recursion → O(log N) depth, classic area/delay knob.

  • Two open questions I want to chase next:

  • Where bsc -verilog actually emits the G/P lookahead trees.
  • Whether Maybe Bit#(1) is the right shape for an overflow-aware carry-out signal.

Next step: testbench + waveform diff against the lab's reference RippleCarry module.

Signed-off-by: Leo Cheng <chengkelfanke@gmail.com>

+34 −0 1A · 0M · 0D 00:03 6.1910 1e47ae2 tests: the ci, LGTM!!!

Round-2 verification of issue-comment layout:

  • horizontal badge row (Branch/Time/Hash on one source line)
  • title now ## (H2) for stronger contrast against body
  • Changes section properly omitted when commit is empty
  • new Files col format +A/$M/−D ready for non-empty commits

Signed-off-by: heke1228 <chengkelfan@qq.com>

+0 −0 0A · 0M · 0D May 11, 2026 23:51 6.1910 b99b5bb tests: ci tested LGTM!

Verifying end-to-end pipeline:

  • centered tables on README & gh-pages recent_table
  • comment renders title (####) / body (raw md) / trailers (>) separately
  • Branch/Time/Hash badges clickable via wrap

    Signed-off-by: heke1228 <chengkelfan@qq.com>

    +0 −0 0A · 0M · 0D 19:40 6.1910 6a58165 tests: change something to trigger ci.

    Signed-off-by: heke1228 <chengkelfan@qq.com>

    +50 −3 1A · 1M · 0D 15:31 6.1910 be3111b init: learning branch for MIT 6.1910.

Constructive Computer Architecture · 6.175  →  6.1920 · Resource · Branch

5 commit(s) on branch 6.1920. A commit titled completed: all done. marks the course as finished.

May 13, 2026 23:37 6.1920 2550d13 baseline: v0.0.1 — env ready, learn...

Signed-off-by: heke1228 <chengkelfan@qq.com>

+0 −0 0A · 0M · 0D 19:12 6.1920 f005a41 fixed: readme info... +2 −2 0A · 1M · 0D 18:41 6.1920 95b4066 init: learning environment

Set up the lab workspace for MIT 6.175 / 6.1920 — Constructive Computer Architecture, 2016 Fall edition.

  • labs/ — 7 labs + final project:
  • lab-1 Multiplexers · Adders · Barrel shifter
  • lab-2 FFT · FIFO · EHR
  • lab-3 Multipliers (combinational / sequential / Booth)
  • lab-4 MyFifo — concurrent FIFO with EHR
  • lab-5 Single-cycle RISC-V + RISC-V test programs
  • lab-6 Pipelined RISC-V
  • lab-7 RISC-V + caches

  • Proj Project 1 + Project 2 (combined workspace, parameterized by CORENUM) — pipelined RISC-V with caches & branch prediction (P1), multicore + cache coherence (P2)

  • notes/ — personal study notes (.gitkeep placeholder).

  • .gitignore — ignore stray dotfiles, keep .git* tracked.
  • README.md — entry, layout, build recipe.

Build any lab with:

cd labs/lab-1 && make

Official course site: https://csg.csail.mit.edu/6.175/.

Signed-off-by: heke1228 <chengkelfan@qq.com>

+46862 −5 499A · 1M · 0D May 11, 2026 23:24 6.1920 e32d8a7 tests: change something to trigger ci.

Signed-off-by: heke1228 <chengkelfan@qq.com>

+3 −1 0A · 1M · 0D 15:31 6.1920 0ac5192 init: learning branch for MIT 6.1920.

Signed-off-by: heke1228 <chengkelfan@qq.com>

+45 −0 1A · 0M · 0D

Computer System Architecture · 6.375  →  6.5900 · Resource · Branch

4 commit(s) on branch 6.5900. A commit titled completed: all done. marks the course as finished.

May 13, 2026 23:39 6.5900 8d717c8 baseline: v0.0.1 — env ready, learn...

Signed-off-by: heke1228 <chengkelfan@qq.com>

+0 −0 0A · 0M · 0D 19:14 6.5900 d9e3378 fixed: readme info. +1 −1 0A · 1M · 0D 18:43 6.5900 f437f88 init: learning environment

Set up the lab workspace for MIT 6.375 / 6.5900 — Computer System Architecture, 2019 edition.

  • labs/ — 5 labs:

  • lab1 Design-only paper lab — sequential modules, scheduling intuition (PDF only)

  • lab2 Audio chain harness — Chunker / FFT / IFFT framework

  • lab3 Pitch adjustment on the audio chain
  • lab4 Connectal — FPGA host / device co-design

  • lab5 RISC-V with multi-level caches, branch prediction, FPGA-deployable

  • notes/ — personal study notes (.gitkeep placeholder).

  • .gitignore — ignore stray dotfiles, keep .git* tracked.
  • README.md — entry, layout, build recipe.

Build any harness with:

cd labs/lab2/audio/fft && make

Official labs: https://csg.csail.mit.edu/6.375/6_375_2019_www/handouts/labs/.

Signed-off-by: heke1228 <chengkelfan@qq.com>

+16578 −3 170A · 1M · 0D May 11, 2026 15:31 6.5900 bce5409 init: learning branch for MIT 6.5900.

Signed-off-by: heke1228 <chengkelfan@qq.com>

+45 −0 1A · 0M · 0D
